metaTitle: Free-Entry Beach Clubs in Bali: What 'Free' Really Means (2026) | Other Bali
title: Free-Entry Beach Clubs in Bali: What 'Free' Really Means (2026)
description: Which Bali beach clubs you can walk into without a ticket — and why 'free entry' still means a seat minimum-spend. Honest guide: Potato Head, FINNS, Single Fin, Kempinski Reef. Checked July 2026.
sub: Where you can walk in for free — and where 'free entry' quietly means a daybed minimum spend. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: 'Free' almost never means a free day in Bali — and it rarely means a free swim. The honest pattern: commercial beach clubs (Potato Head, FINNS, Single Fin, WooBar) let you walk in without a ticket, but any daybed or table carries a minimum spend (roughly IDR 300,000–1,000,000) you redeem against food and drink — so you only pay if you claim a seat, and a bar stool or general beanbag can cost just a drink. Hotel pools are different: most five-star resorts do not let you simply walk in and swim — you book ahead and pay a day-pass or minimum spend. The only ones we could confirm as genuinely free are Segara Village's Byrd House in Sanur (free, no minimum) and The Apurva Kempinski's Reef Beach Club (free entry and free pool — but you now book ahead). Everywhere else, read 'free' as free to enter, pay for a seat.
callout: 'Free entry' ≠ free swim. A hotel pool you can walk into and use for nothing is rare — nearly every venue below charges a seat minimum-spend, and hotel pools need a booking. Read every 'free' here as free to enter, pay for a seat, and confirm the current policy with the venue before promising it to a guest — these terms change.
checkedNote: Prices marked reported come from recent press — confirm at booking. ++ = ~21% tax & service; “net” = price minus redeemable F&B credit.
tableRows: No minimum — order à la carte; small towel fee for the pool. Confirmed free (Jul 2026)

faq.q_text: Which Bali beach clubs are actually free?
faq.a_html: Two we could confirm as genuinely free with no minimum: Segara Village's Byrd House in Sanur, and The Apurva Kempinski's Reef Beach Club (free entry and pool — but you now book ahead). The big commercial clubs — Potato Head, FINNS, Single Fin, La Brisa, The Lawn — are free to enter, but any daybed or table carries a minimum spend. Most hotel pools need a booking and a minimum. Always confirm current terms.
faq.a_text: Two we could confirm as genuinely free with no minimum: Segara Village's Byrd House in Sanur, and The Apurva Kempinski's Reef Beach Club (free entry and pool — but you now book ahead). The big commercial clubs — Potato Head, FINNS, Single Fin, La Brisa, The Lawn — are free to enter, but any daybed or table carries a minimum spend. Most hotel pools need a booking and a minimum. Always confirm current terms.

faq.q_text: Does 'free entry' mean a free day at the beach club?
faq.a_html: No. At most clubs the beanbags and daybeds carry a minimum spend that you redeem against food and drink, so you still pay for whatever you order. You can usually stand at the bar or take a general seat for just the price of a drink.
faq.a_text: No. At most clubs the beanbags and daybeds carry a minimum spend that you redeem against food and drink, so you still pay for whatever you order. You can usually stand at the bar or take a general seat for just the price of a drink.

faq.q_text: Which free beach club has the best sunset?
faq.a_html: Single Fin in Uluwatu (free to enter, seat minimum, packed on Sunday) and The Lawn in Canggu. For a free daytime pool, Kempinski Reef in Nusa Dua.
faq.a_text: Single Fin in Uluwatu (free to enter, seat minimum, packed on Sunday) and The Lawn in Canggu. For a free daytime pool, Kempinski Reef in Nusa Dua.

faq.q_text: Is Potato Head free?
faq.a_html: Yes for most of the day. There's an IDR 200,000 sunset cover charge from about 16:00–18:30 that's redeemable against food and drink.
faq.a_text: Yes for most of the day. There's an IDR 200,000 sunset cover charge from about 16:00–18:30 that's redeemable against food and drink.

metaTitle: Bali Hotel Sunday Brunches: Every District Compared (2026) | Other Bali
title: Bali Hotel Sunday Brunches: Every District Compared (2026)
description: The complete Bali hotel Sunday-brunch guide: beachfront and jungle resort brunches by district, prices per adult, and what's included. All areas. Checked July 2026.
h1: Bali hotel Sunday brunches, by district
sub: The island's resort Sunday brunches — price per adult, what's included, and how to book. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: A Bali resort Sunday brunch is a whole afternoon — buffet or à la carte, usually with pool or beach access, from about IDR 400,000 per adult (COMO Uma in Ubud) to the grand seafood spreads at St Regis and Mulia in Nusa Dua (IDR 1.1–1.65M). Free-flow tiers push higher. Below is every district with a brunch scene; open a guide for the full priced list. Most take outside guests by reservation and run roughly Sunday 12:00–15:30.
callout: Reserve by Thursday. The popular resort brunches — St Regis, Kempinski, W Bali, Four Seasons Sundara — sell out for Sunday, especially in high season. Book mid-week and ask what's non-alcoholic vs free-flow, since that's the biggest price swing.
checkedNote: Prices marked reported come from recent press — confirm at booking. ++ = ~21% tax & service; “net” = price minus redeemable F&B credit.

metaTitle: Bali Resort Day Passes: Every District Compared (2026) | Other Bali
title: Bali Resort Day Passes: Every District Compared (2026)
description: The complete Bali resort day-pass guide: pool and beach-club passes by district, starting prices, and which resorts to book direct instead of Klook. All 7 areas. Checked July 2026.
h1: Bali resort day passes, by district
sub: Use a five-star pool or beach club for a day — every district, starting prices, and where to book direct. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: You don't need to stay at a Bali resort to use its pool or beach — most sell a day pass, and much of the price often comes back as food-and-drink credit. Passes start around IDR 150,000 (Ayodya in Nusa Dua, Westin in Ubud) and climb to a IDR 1.7M room-included day at Hilton or Bulgari's luxury day in Uluwatu. Below is every district; open a guide for the full priced list. One rule island-wide: in Sanur and parts of Nusa Dua the best passes are direct-only — not on Klook or the DayPass app — so booking direct avoids markup.
callout: Coverage, not commission. We list day passes so you can plan a pool day in one place; we don't take a cut of them. Prices are checked to the date shown and change seasonally — always confirm the current rate and F&B credit at booking.
checkedNote: Prices marked reported come from recent press — confirm at booking. ++ = ~21% tax & service; “net” = price minus redeemable F&B credit.

W Bali WooBar; free-entry beach clubs
Griya Santrian; all direct-only, none on apps
The Edge Oneeighty glass cliff pool

faq.a_html: From about IDR 150,000 (Ayodya in Nusa Dua, Westin in Ubud) up to IDR 1,700,000 for Hilton's room-included day. Most mid-range five-star pools sit around IDR 300,000–600,000, much of it redeemable as food and drink.
faq.a_text: From about IDR 150,000 (Ayodya in Nusa Dua, Westin in Ubud) up to IDR 1,700,000 for Hilton's room-included day. Most mid-range five-star pools sit around IDR 300,000–600,000, much of it redeemable as food and drink.

faq.a_html: In Sanur, every resort day pass is direct-only. In Nusa Dua, Ayodya, Merusaka, Nusa Dua Beach Hotel and Hilton also sell only via WhatsApp or their own sites. Booking direct avoids aggregator markup.

metaTitle: Bali's Best Sunset Clubs by Area: Prices, Minimum Spend & Booking | Other Bali
title: Bali's Best Sunset Clubs by Area: Prices, Minimum Spend & Booking
description: Bali's best sunset clubs and dinners, by area — Uluwatu cliffs, Jimbaran seafood, Seminyak & Canggu beach clubs. Prices, minimum spends and how to book. Sanur is sunrise, not sunset. Checked July 2026.
h1: Bali's best sunset clubs, by area
sub: The whole west coast, ranked and explained — with prices, minimum spends and where to book. (Sanur faces east — that's sunrise.) Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: Bali's west coast is the sunset coast: Uluwatu's cliffs, Jimbaran's seafood beach, and Seminyak & Canggu's beach clubs. Nearly all are free (or cheap) to enter with a redeemable seat minimum spend — roughly IDR 150,000–400,000 per person at a table, more for a daybed. For the view it's One Eighty's glass cliff-pool (Uluwatu); for atmosphere Single Fin (Uluwatu) and Potato Head (Seminyak); for a beach it's Sundays (Uluwatu) or Jimbaran's seafood sand. Sanur faces east, so it's sunrise, not sunset.
callout: The sunset rule, island-wide. Book a front-row seat or daybed 1–7 days ahead (more for weekends and full moon), arrive by ~16:30, and check each venue's seat minimum spend — it's redeemable on F&B, not an entry fee. Traffic to the Bukit (Uluwatu/Jimbaran) is heavy at sunset — leave early.
checkedNote: Minimum spends are redeemable on food & drink, not entry fees. ++ = ~21% tax & service. verify = press-sourced/unpublished — confirm at booking.
tableRows: The glass cliff-pool, 162 m up — the view
The original clifftop surf-bar; Sun/Wed parties
Cliff bar 14 m above the sea (non-guests 19:30+)
Grilled seafood on the sand at sunset

faq.q_text: Where is the best sunset in Bali?
faq.a_html: The west coast. For the view, One Eighty's glass cliff-pool at The Edge (Uluwatu); for atmosphere, Single Fin (Uluwatu) or Potato Head (Seminyak); for a beach, Sundays Beach Club (Uluwatu) or Jimbaran's seafood sand.
faq.a_text: The west coast. For the view, One Eighty's glass cliff-pool at The Edge (Uluwatu); for atmosphere, Single Fin (Uluwatu) or Potato Head (Seminyak); for a beach, Sundays Beach Club (Uluwatu) or Jimbaran's seafood sand.

faq.q_text: How much do Bali sunset clubs cost?
faq.a_html: Most are free or cheap to enter but charge a redeemable seat minimum spend — roughly IDR 150,000–400,000 per person at a table, more for daybeds and cabanas. It's spent on food and drinks, not an entry fee.
faq.a_text: Most are free or cheap to enter but charge a redeemable seat minimum spend — roughly IDR 150,000–400,000 per person at a table, more for daybeds and cabanas. It's spent on food and drinks, not an entry fee.

faq.q_text: Can you watch sunset in Sanur?
faq.a_html: Sanur faces east, so it gets sunrise, not sunset — it's the spot for an early-morning beach walk. For sunset, head to the west coast.
faq.a_text: Sanur faces east, so it gets sunrise, not sunset — it's the spot for an early-morning beach walk. For sunset, head to the west coast.

faq.q_text: Which area is easiest for a first-timer?
faq.a_html: Seminyak and Canggu beach clubs are the most walk-up-friendly; Uluwatu and Jimbaran have the most dramatic settings but need more of a plan (traffic, booking ahead).
faq.a_text: Seminyak and Canggu beach clubs are the most walk-up-friendly; Uluwatu and Jimbaran have the most dramatic settings but need more of a plan (traffic, booking ahead).

title: Canggu Beach Club & Pool Day Passes: 2026 Prices
description: Canggu hotel beach-club and pool day passes compared: adult price, F&B credit and how to book. COMO Uma, Vue, Swarga. Plus the free-entry beach clubs. Checked July 2026.
sub: Hotel beach clubs and pools by the day — price, credit and how to book. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: Canggu's hotel day passes run from about IDR 175,000 to 800,000 per adult. The value pick is Vue Beach Club (~IDR 175,000); the boutique pick is Swarga Berawa (IDR 350,000 net); the luxury one is COMO Uma's beach club (IDR 500,000) or full-resort pass (IDR 800,000). Note Canggu's giant beach clubs — FINNS, Atlas, Vue — mostly run their own entry/daybed systems rather than a hotel pass.
callout: Hotel pass vs beach-club daybed: COMO Uma and Swarga sell a true hotel day pass (fixed price, often creditable). The mega beach clubs (FINNS, Atlas Beach Fest) use their own ticketing and daybed minimums — different product, booked on their own engines or Klook.

answer: Jimbaran resort day passes run from about IDR 211,750 to 2,500,000 per adult (the top figure is Sundara's daybed for two). metaTitle: Jimbaran Sunset Seafood: Beach BBQ Prices & Where to Sit | Other Bali
title: Jimbaran Sunset Seafood: Beach BBQ Prices & Where to Sit
description: Jimbaran grilled-seafood-on-the-sand sunset dinners compared: set prices, front-row scarcity, plus AYANA Rock Bar and Sundara. Checked July 2026.
sub: Grilled seafood on the sand as the sun goes down — set prices, where to sit and how to get a front-row table. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: Jimbaran's sunset is grilled seafood eaten at tables on the sand. Set dinners run roughly IDR 120,000–500,000 per person at the beach warungs (Teba, Bawang Merah, Menega), with 2-person sets to IDR 1.5–2.6M. Above the beach, AYANA's Rock Bar is the cliff sunset icon (seat minimum spend from IDR 300,000) and Sundara at Four Seasons is the polished beach club. Front-row sand tables at sunset are scarce — arrive by 16:30 or WhatsApp ahead.
callout: The sunset rule. The seafood warungs are walk-in but front-row tables on the sand go fast around 16:30–17:00 — WhatsApp to hold one. AYANA keeps 16:00–17:00 at Rock Bar for hotel guests; non-guests are seated from 19:30, so for the actual sunset drink book early or pick a beach warung.

title: Nusa Dua Hotel Sunday Brunches: 2026 Prices & What's Included
description: Every Sunday brunch at Nusa Dua's 5-star resorts, compared: price per adult, what's included, pool access, hours and how to book. Prices checked July 2026.
sub: Every 5-star Sunday brunch in Nusa Dua, compared — price, what's included, pool access & how to book. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: Nusa Dua's hotel Sunday brunches run from about IDR 650,000 to 1,650,000 per adult (non-alcoholic; free-flow alcohol adds roughly IDR 450,000–1,100,000). The best value is Apurva Kempinski's Brunchcation at IDR 798,000++ — it bundles the buffet plus pool, waterslide and beach access, so it doubles as a day pass. For the highest-end spread, Kayuputi at The St. Regis is the marquee (from IDR 1,250,000). Kids are typically half price and under-6 free. Most brunches run Sunday 11:00–15:00 and require a reservation.

The best-known Sunday brunch on the island: a vast Mediterranean and Pan-Asian buffet with a live grill and seafood bar. Reported around IDR 1,136,000 per adult (confirm live on EatApp — the resort doesn't publish the figure).

metaTitle: Sanur Resort Day Passes: 2026 Prices, Pools & What's Included | Other Bali
title: Sanur Resort Day Passes: 2026 Prices, Pools & What's Included
description: Every Sanur 4-5 star resort day pass compared: entry price, F&B credit, pool & beach access, hours and how to book. Prices checked July 2026. Book direct — none are on Klook.
sub: Every 4–5 star day pass in Sanur, compared — entry price, F&B credit, pool & beach access and how to book. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: Sanur's resort day passes range from free entry (pay only for food & drink) to about IDR 700,000 per adult — but most bundle an F&B credit that offsets the entry, so the real spend is lower. The smartest value is Griya Santrian at IDR 500,000, of which IDR 475,000 comes back as a food-and-drink credit — a net ~IDR 25,000 for a beachfront pool day. For zero entry, Byrd House Beach Club (Segara Village) is free — you just spend on F&B. Kids are typically half price and under-6 free.
callout: Book these direct. Unlike Nusa Dua, none of Sanur's resort day passes are sold on Klook or the DayPass app — you reserve them straight with the hotel (WhatsApp, phone or website), usually a day ahead. Prices below are what the hotels quote.

metaTitle: Seminyak Pool & Beach-Club Day Passes: 2026 Prices | Other Bali
title: Seminyak Pool & Beach-Club Day Passes: 2026 Prices
description: Seminyak hotel pool and beach-club day passes compared: entry, daybed min-spend and how to book. W Bali, Hotel Indigo SugarSand, Peppers, U Paasha. Checked July 2026.
sub: Hotel pools and beach clubs by the day — entry, daybed minimums and how to book. Prices checked 19 Jul 2026 · resident-curated, no paid placement.
answer: Seminyak splits two ways. Hotel pool day passes are cheap and fixed — from about IDR 250,000 (Peppers, U Paasha). The big beach clubs (W Bali's WooBar, Hotel Indigo's SugarSand) mostly use a free-entry, daybed-minimum-spend model rather than a ticket, so your cost depends on the seat you pick. Grand Seminyak sells a pool + afternoon-tea pass (~US$31).
callout: Two pricing models: a fixed pool pass (Peppers, U Paasha, Grand Seminyak) has one price you pay whatever you order; a beach-club daybed (W, SugarSand) is free to enter but the seat carries a minimum spend. Pick by how much you plan to eat and drink.

faq.a_text: From about IDR 300,000 (Six Senses) to 750,000 (Alila Villas) per adult. The Edge's Oneeighty glass sky-pool is IDR 700,000; Bulgari's curated day is a separate IDR 5,000,000++ per couple.

faq.q_text: Which Uluwatu pool is easiest to book online?
faq.a_html: Anantara — it's the only Uluwatu clifftop resort live on the DayPass app (IDR 500,000). Others book by hotel email, Chope or concierge.
faq.a_text: Anantara — it's the only Uluwatu clifftop resort live on the DayPass app (IDR 500,000). Others book by hotel email, Chope or concierge.

faq.q_text: Is the price redeemable as food and drink?
faq.a_html: Largely, yes — Six Senses (~230k credit), Oneeighty (600k credit) and Alila (350k credit) return much of the pass as F&B credit.
faq.a_text: Largely, yes — Six Senses (~230k credit), Oneeighty (600k credit) and Alila (350k credit) return much of the pass as F&B credit.

faq.q_text: What's the difference from the sunset clubs?
faq.a_html: These are daytime pool passes. For sunset drinks at Single Fin, El Kabron, Sundays and Savaya, see the Uluwatu sunset-clubs guide.

jsonld.headline: Uluwatu clifftop pool day passes: 2026 prices & credit
